text.title: a11y-basics Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for a11y-basics as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Adding aria-label to a native element that already has an accessible name from its label element.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Duplicate accessible names confuse screen readers; the aria-label overrides the visible label, causing inconsistency for sighted screen-reader users.

failure-mode.description: Using role="button" on a div element.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Native <button> provides keyboard activation (Enter and Space), implicit role, and focus management for free; div + role requires manual keyboard handler and is frequently incomplete.

failure-mode.description: Adding ARIA attributes to fix scanner failures without understanding the semantic model.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Bad ARIA is worse than no ARIA; it creates a false accessible name or role that misleads assistive technology into announcing incorrect information.
